content: Press enter or click to view image in full size Member-only story 4 min read 8 hours ago -- 1. Switch from absolute URL strings to parameterized routerLink arrays for dynamic routes String concatenation is error prone. Array syntax ensures Angular correctly encodes path segments and reduces malformed URLs when inserting dynamic values. Example: Current User2. Update query parameters via router.navigate with queryParamsHandling: ‘merge’ Merging ensures only the intended parameter changes while preserving the rest of the URL state. Example: Suppose the user is still on the product list and changes only the sort order. - You want to keep category and brand. - You only want to update sort. Current URL: /products?category=laptops&brand=dell&sort=pricerouter.navigate( ['/products'], { queryParams: { sort: 'rating' }, queryParamsHandling: 'merge' } );Result: /products?category=laptops&brand=dell&sort=rating
